title: C++ Comments
content:
### Comments in C++

Comments are non-executable lines used to explain code, provide documentation, or temporarily disable code.

---

### Single-Line Comments

Use `//` to comment out everything to the end of the line:

```cpp
// This is a single-line comment
int x = 5; // This is an inline comment
```

---

### Multi-Line Comments

Use `/* */` for blocks spanning multiple lines:

```cpp
/*
  This is a multi-line comment.
  It can span several lines.
  Useful for detailed explanations.
*/
int y = 10;
```

---

### Documentation Comments

C++ supports documentation comments using `///` or `/** */`:

```cpp
/// Calculates the sum of two integers.
/// @param a First number
/// @param b Second number
/// @return Sum of a and b
int add(int a, int b) {
  return a + b;
}
```

---

| Comment Type | Syntax | Use Case |
|-------------|--------|----------|
| Single-line | `// text` | Quick explanations |
| Multi-line | `/* text */` | Block descriptions |
| Documentation | `///` or `/** */` | API documentation |

---

> **Key Point:** Comments are ignored by the compiler. They exist solely for human readers. Well-commented code is easier to maintain and debug.

quiz.question: Which symbol starts a single-line comment in C++?
